narrative:
DRIVER
GBP leads H4 CSM at 100 with a 41-point divergence over D1 (59), flagging this as the single most important short-term signal: GBP is accelerating while USD fades from D1 CSM 100 to H4 57. GBPUSD and GBPJPY are the cleanest expressions, with GBPUSD correlation at +0.92 to GBPJPY meaning both move together and amplify any GBP long thesis. USD D1 CSM 100 versus H4 57 means USDCAD D1 buy signals are stale and USDJPY H4 buy at +4 fights a fading USD — treat both with reduced conviction.

MOMENTUM
Gaining: GBPUSD H4 67 rising sharply while D1 39 fades, GBPJPY H4 70 rising, NZDUSD H4 58 rising with D1 53 stable
Fading: USDCAD H4 48 falling hard against D1 74, CADJPY H4 58 falling, USDCHF H4 41 fading

WATCH
Fed speakers referencing rate path hit USDJPY first given H4 buy signal at +4 conflicting with fading USD CSM, a hawkish tone extends the move above current range. WTI oil at 103.3 down 4.94 percent directly pressures CADJPY lower given CAD CSM at zero on D1. RBA commentary referencing China slowdown triggers AUDUSD to resume H4 sell at -3 with AUDJPY correlated at 0.91 confirming the move.

themes.theme: USD strength amid Fed rate hike expectations
Oil price surge supporting commodity currencies
EUR weakness amid geopolitical uncertainty and oil prices
AUD under pressure from RBA pause and China slowdown
Iran tensions and Middle East conflict driving safe-haven demand
NZD easing in cautious risk environment
BoC rate decision expectations shaping CAD dynamics

key_observation: USD strengthening on Fed rate hike bets while geopolitical tensions from Iran situation support safe-haven flows. Oil prices surge above 107 USD per barrel benefiting CAD and NOK, but broader risk-off sentiment weighs on risk currencies AUD and NZD. JPY surprisingly weak despite geopolitical premium suggesting carry trade dynamics dominate.
watch: Iran headlines for market direction, Canadian CPI data impact on CAD, EUR break below 1.1600 support level, USD Index 99.40 resistance, oil price stability above 107, central bank policy divergence between Fed, BoC and RBA
